meaning: n.吗哪(<<圣经>>故事所述,古以色列人经过荒野所得的天赐食物),精神食粮,天赐,甘露
examples: Downloading music from the Internet has been manna from heaven for some consumers.
The bingeing US consumer was Asia's manna from heaven.
Your fathers did eat manna in the wilderness, and are dead.
The supporting cast is uniformly excellent, with Manna Nichols turning in a Broadway-class performance as Tuptim.
The case of the missing intern is manna from heaven for the new-look tabloids.
